qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary file write via path traversal in MCPB package upload
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function (mcpbController.ts uploadMcpbFile), same sink (path.join at line 95/107 using unsanitized manifest.name), same root cause (no validation of path traversal sequences in manifest name field before constructing filesystem paths for mkdirSync and renameSync), same CWE-22 path traversal class.

selected_finding.title: Arbitrary file write via path traversal in MCPB package upload

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The finding identifies the exact same vulnerable code path as the advisory: src/controllers/mcpbController.ts where manifest.name from an uploaded MCPB file is used directly in path.join() without sanitization (line 95 in v0.12.12, line 107 in advisory reference), enabling path traversal via '../' sequences. An end-to-end HTTP PoC was executed: a minimal Express server running the vulnerable mcpbController logic from @samanhappy/mcphub@0.12.12 accepted a malicious MCPB upload via POST /api/mcpb/upload with manifest.name='../../../pwned-by-mcpb', and the server wrote a file containing attacker-controlled content (ARBITRARY_FILE_WRITE_SUCCESS) to data/uploads/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt, which is outside the intended data/uploads/mcpb/ directory. This satisfies exploit_success: the advisory-described path traversal vulnerability causes arbitrary file write through the MCPB upload endpoint.
evidence: Vulnerable code confirmed in @samanhappy/mcphub@0.12.12 mcpbController.js line 95: const finalExtractDir = path.join(path.dirname(mcpbFilePath), `server-${manifest.name}`) with no sanitization of manifest.name
End-to-end HTTP PoC via POST /api/mcpb/upload returned success:true with is_outside_upload_dir:true
Proof file written to data/uploads/pwned-by-mcpb/proof.txt (outside intended data/uploads/mcpb/) containing ARBITRARY_FILE_WRITE_SUCCESS
Patched version 1.0.12 adds validateMcpbServerName() with regex check and resolveMcpbServerExtractDir() with path escape detection, confirming the fix addresses exactly this vulnerability

poc_executions.observed_effect: HTTP POST /api/mcpb/upload with malicious MCPB (manifest.name='../../../pwned-by-mcpb') caused server to write proof.txt containing 'ARBITRARY_FILE_WRITE_SUCCESS' to data/uploads/pwned-by-mcpb/, outside the intended data/uploads/mcpb/ directory. Server response confirmed: success=true, is_outside_upload_dir=true, proof_content=ARBITRARY_FILE_WRITE_SUCCESS.
